summary: Dedicates up to $400,000 annually of fees collected at Island Beach and Liberty State Parks to special funds to be used to support certain park programs, and makes appropriations from funds.

summary: Requires candidates for Governor, Lieutenant Governor, State Senate, and General Assembly to disclose federal income tax returns.

title: Legislature-provides for public comment period at cert session of each house

summary: Raises mandatory retirement age for SPRS members from 55 to 60.

summary: Prohibits entity that receives grant from New Jersey State Council on Arts from scheduling educational courses on days when local school district is closed for religious holiday.

title: Governor & Legislative memb-expends deadline,  filing finan disclosure statement
summary: Expands deadline for filing financial disclosure statements by candidates for Governor and member of Legislature.

title: Dredging control reserve fund-authorizes municipalities to establish

summary: Allows law enforcement officers in PERS to transfer to and enroll in PFRS; requires all law enforcement officers to be enrolled in PFRS.

summary: Allows judgment for restitution of stolen public funds to be levied against convicted employee's retirement allowance or pension contributions.

summary: Establishes civil action for damages against organizations that support terrorism.

summary: Imposes mandatory term of life imprisonment without eligibility for parole on persons who commit particularly brutal sexual assaults.

title: Greenhead fly population-authorizes counties to reduce;$50K
summary: Authorizes counties to reduce greenhead fly populations; appropriates $50,000.

title: Office of State Dental Director and NJ Oral Health Commission-establishes
summary: Establishes Office of State Dental Director and New Jersey Oral Health Commission.

summary: Modifies "New Jersey Tort Claims Act" to provide for State to be strictly liable for injuries caused by State-mandated vaccines.

summary: Requires informed consent for breast implant surgery.

title: Beneficiary designation-allows retiree to change, after retirement and divorce
summary: Allows retiree to change designated beneficiary after retirement in event of divorce.

summary: Allows certain persons with developmental disabilities 21 years of age and older to attend special education programs and to simultaneously participate in adult day and employment programs.

summary: Requires certain disclosures by providers of commercial financing.

title: Internet-requires State agencies to review operations and implement use
summary: Requires State agencies to review operations and to implement use of Internet to conduct public business when cost savings may be realized, with certain exceptions.

title: Property tax appeals, third-party-prohibits
summary: Prohibits certain third-party property tax appeals.

summary: Provides for identification and study of infant fatalities and near fatalities resulting from vaccination; requires inclusion of vaccination information in sudden infant death reports; and requires use of federal infant death reporting form.

title: Children's Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System-establishes
summary: Establishes Children's Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System.

summary: Creates database of veteran separation documents.

primary_sponsors: Bramnick, Jon M.; Cryan, Joseph P.

summary: Authorizes Surrogate of every county to establish electronic systems to accept electronic signatures on qualifying documents in probate matters.

summary: Requires exterior exit doors in school buildings to remain unlocked from egress side and locked from access side at all times.

summary: Permits patient visitation in licensed health care facilities if consistent with federal or State law.

title: Health info, personal-proh cert entity from requesting or requiring provision of
summary: Prohibits certain entities from requesting or requiring provision of personal health information.

summary: Prohibits mandatory mask use in licensed child care centers.

summary: Prohibits mandatory mask use at youth camps.

summary: Requires long-term care facilities to submit reports regarding residents with disabilities.

title: Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion, Department of-establishes in Executive Branch
summary: Establishes Department of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion as principal department in Executive Branch and specifies functions, powers, and duties of department.

summary: Revises distribution of monies from Alcohol Education, Rehabilitation and Enforcement Fund to municipalities.

summary: Requires Director of Division of Taxation to include sales of properties in age-restricted developments by third parties in table of equalized valuations.

summary: Requires explosive gas detectors to be installed in certain residential properties.

summary: Provides that 1.25 percent tax on sports wagering revenues be used for local tax relief purposes for Atlantic City.

summary: Establishes the "Mortgage Assistance Pilot Program."

summary: Provides gross income tax deduction for home office expenses incurred during COVID-19 pandemic.

summary: Prohibits sale, distribution, and use of urea as an ice melt.

summary: "Barnegat Bay Protection Act"; establishes Barnegat Bay Protection Fund, dedicates portion of sales tax on fertilizer, authorizes special license plates, and provides for donations.

summary: Changes law regulating size limits of lobsters.

title: Pet adoptions-waives certain fees for veterans

summary: Requires State Health Benefits Commission advertise and annually review NJWELL program.

summary: Permits board of education to accept donations for purpose of providing property tax relief.

summary: Requires certain consumer contracts to include translations to languages other than English.

summary: Establishes immunity for senior planned real estate development associations relating to COVID-19.

summary: Permits county board of elections to open inner envelopes containing mail-in ballots up to five days prior to election day.

title: Infrastructure-permits counties to charge a fee to fund through voter approval
summary: Permits counties to charge a fee to fund infrastructure through voter approval.

summary: Expands duties of State Auditor to include performance audits at request of legislator; establishes Performance Audit Committee, consisting of eight public members, to help conduct performance audits.

summary: Establishes certification card for individuals diagnosed with autism spectrum disorder.

summary: Requires that newly-purchased school buses be equipped with air conditioning and heating systems.

summary: Requires settlement agreements concerning equal employment opportunity to be approved by NJT board of directors.

summary: Requires NJT to adopt plan to evacuate passengers when rail passenger vehicle becomes immobilized in tunnel.

title: Affordability controls, rental housing-establish standards for expiration
summary: Establishes standards for expiration of rental housing affordability controls.

summary: Requires State agencies to submit notice of request for proposals or other documents pertinent to privatization contracts to certain State employees.

title: Automobile insurance practices-concerns
summary: Concerns automobile insurance practices.

title: Income thresholds-increase minimum income, requiring filing & paying income tax
summary: Increases minimum income thresholds requiring filing and paying of gross income tax.

title: Insurance coverage, complex medical needs-requires for persons 18 or younger
summary: Requires health insurance carriers to provide coverage for persons 18 or younger with diagnosed complex medical needs.

title: Housing codes-enhances penalties upon corporate violators
summary: Enhances penalties upon corporate violators of housing codes.

title: Demographic info on corp boards/senior mgt-req collection & study, St. Treasurer
summary: Requires State Treasurer to collect and study certain demographic information on corporate boards and in senior management.

title: Correctional facility inmates who report sexual abuse-prohibits retaliation
summary: Prohibits retaliation against inmates in State correctional facilities who report sexual abuse; criminalizes retaliation and failure to report abuse.

title: Domestic violence restraining orders-include pregnant victim's child upon birth
summary: Authorizes court to include in domestic violence restraining orders a provision making the order applicable to a pregnant victim's child upon birth of the child.

title: Restraining order-issue for situations where domestic viol statutes do not apply
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Gopal, Vin; Bramnick, Jon M.
summary: Authorizes issuance of protective orders for certain victimized persons in situations for which domestic violence statutes do not apply due to lack of familial or dating relationship between victim and offending actor.*

title: Victims of domestic violence, sexual assault or stalking-prov. rental/lease prot
summary: Provides rental and lease protections for victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking.

title: Disabled persons-estab civil action for abuse, neglect, exploitation, bullying
summary: Establishes civil action for abuse, neglect, exploitation or bullying of minor or adult with disability; provides that bullying of vulnerable adult may constitute grounds for investigation by adult protective services and report to law enforcement.

title: Pharmacy Audit Bill of Rights-estab. procedures by which entities conduct audits
summary: "New Jersey Pharmacy Audit Bill of Rights;" establishes procedures by which entities are required to conduct audits of pharmacies.

title: Mammograms-req prof order bi-lateral ultrasounds concurrently; req insurer cover
summary: Requires health care professionals to order bi-lateral ultrasounds concurrently when ordering mammograms; requires insurers to cover concurrent mammograms and bilateral ultrasounds.

title: Nurses, advanced practice-eliminates certain practice restrictions for
summary: Eliminates certain practice restrictions for advanced practice nurses.

title: Bisphenol A-prohibits use of receipt paper containing
summary: Prohibits use of receipt paper containing bisphenol A.

title: Food waste in schools & higher education instit.-requires DEP update guidelines
summary: Requires DEP to update school food waste guidelines to include recommendations for how to better utilize food nearing best-by and expiration dates.

title: Pollinator habitat, native, on undeveloped property-prov CBT credit to taxpayers
summary: Provides corporation business tax credit to taxpayers that develop qualified native pollinator habitat on undeveloped property.

title: Electric or renewable power source veh-counties and municipalities purch w/bonds
summary: Permits counties and municipalities to bond for passenger cars and station wagons solely fueled by electric or renewable power sources.

title: Power outages-BPU study microgrid & distributed energy resource deployment
summary: Directs BPU to conduct study to determine whether microgrid and distributed energy resource deployment will reduce length of power outages.

title: Reliability, Preparedness & Storm Response Act-pub. util file emerg preparedness
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Turner, Shirley K.
summary: "Reliability, Preparedness, and Storm Response Act;" requires public utilities to file certain information concerning emergency preparedness with BPU and increases certain penalties.

title: Disaster Victims Protection Act-Governor allocate federal/State disaster aid
summary: "Disaster Victims Protection Act"; requires Governor to allocate federal and State disaster aid to municipalities in proportion to amount of catastrophic physical damage sustained within each municipality.

title: Hazard mitigation plans, State & county-req. climate change threat assessments
summary: Requires hazard mitigation plans to include climate change-related threat assessments and hazard prevention and mitigation strategies.*

title: Billing due date-proh. telecommunications/utility companies from charging prior
summary: Prohibits telecommunications, utility or cable television companies from charging certain customers prior to actual billing due date.

title: Solar and photovoltaic energy facilities and structures on farmland-provides
summary: Provides that solar and photovoltaic energy facilities and structures on farmland are not inherently beneficial use, per se, for purposes of zoning approvals.

title: State building, new-include distributed energy resource, retrofit existing bldgs
summary: Requires State to include distributed energy resource in certain new State buildings and consider retrofitting certain existing State buildings with distributed energy resource.

title: Wastewater system improvement charge-adopt regulations allowing utilities impose
summary: Requires BPU to adopt regulations allowing wastewater utilities to impose wastewater system improvement charge.

title: Airports operated by counties/mun-concerns financing; operate as county utility
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Turner, Shirley K.; Benson, Daniel R.
summary: Permits counties to operate airports as county utilities; provides that bonds for county and municipal airport purposes be issued in accordance with provisions of "Local Bond Law."

title: Veterans, war service disabilities-increase in allowance paid
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Testa, Michael L.
summary: Increases allowance paid to war veterans with certain service-connected disabilities.

title: Vet. disab. compensation-exclude income qualification limit under prop tax reimb
summary: Excludes veteran disability compensation from income qualification limits under homestead property tax reimbursement program.

title: Veterans home purchase-creates grant program for federal active duty
summary: Creates home purchase grant program in HMFA for certain veterans who served in federal active duty; appropriates $2,000,000.

title: Disabled vet. property tax exemption-auth; extends eligibility to mental illness
summary: Authorizes proportional property tax exemption for honorably discharged veterans having service-connected permanent disability; extends eligibility to veterans suffering from mental illness; establishes eligibility of property owned by disabled veteran with surviving partner for exemption.

title: Bonds-auth. local units to refinance irrespective of present value savings, 1 yr
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Turner, Shirley K.
summary: Authorizes local units to refinance general obligation bonds irrespective of present value savings for period of one year.

title: Free parking on election day at polling places-requires availability
summary: Requires availability of free parking at polling places on election day.

title: Polling places-directs Secretary of State to study wait times
summary: Directs Secretary of State to study wait times at polling places during a general election.

title: Developmental disab. person-allow CBT & income tax credit to businesses who emp.
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Corrado, Kristin M.
summary: Allows corporation business tax and gross income tax credits to businesses employing certain persons with developmental disabilities.

title: Rutgers University Foundation-allow gross income tax deduction certain donations
summary: Allows gross income tax deduction for certain donations to the Rutgers University Foundation.

title: Condominium associations-prohibits assessing insurance deductibles to unit owner
summary: Prohibits condominium associations from assessing insurance deductibles to individual unit owners or groups of unit owners.

title: Senior Citizens Prop. Tax Deferral Act-allow seniors to defer prop. tax payments
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Gill, Nia H.
summary: "Senior Citizens Property Tax Deferral Act"; allows certain seniors to defer property tax payments.

title: Municipal liens on residential property-facilitates ID cert seniors/disab person
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Gill, Nia H.
summary: Facilitates identification of municipal liens on residential property of certain seniors and disabled persons.

title: Workers' comp. benefits for certain volunteer & other workers-provide w max benf
summary: Provides certain volunteer and other workers with maximum compensation benefit for workers' compensation claim regardless of outside employment.

title: Payday lending-includes as a violation of the consumer fraud act
summary: Includes payday lending as a violation of the consumer fraud act.

title: Teaching staff input identifiable health info into third party software-prohibit
summary: Prohibits teaching staff members from inputting information and conversations regarding individually identifiable health information into third party software applications managed by entities engaging in partisan political activity.

title: Sexual assault victims-proh court requiring gynecological or cert physical exams
summary: Prohibits court from requiring victim of sexual assault to submit to or undergo gynecological or certain physical examinations.

title: Selective Service part of driv. license application, males under age 26-register
summary: Makes consent to register with Selective Service part of driver's license application process for males under age 26.

title: Teaching certification, alternative-permit State Board of Education to authorize
primary_sponsors: Beach, James; Ruiz, M. Teresa; Lampitt, Pamela R.
summary: Permits State Board of Education to authorize alternative teaching certificate.*

title: Officials at athletic events sanctioned by association-criminal background check
summary: Requires New Jersey State Interscholastic Athletic Association to require criminal history records check for person to serve as official at athletic events sanctioned by association.

title: School Transportation Safety Commission-establishes
summary: Establishes School Transportation Safety Commission in Department of Law and Public Safety.

title: Youth participation in government, encouraging-establish one-time grant
summary: Establishes one-time grant to study and recommend best practices for encouraging youth participation in government.

title: Students w/epilepsy & seizure disorder-ensure all staff trained every 2 years
summary: Requires boards of education to ensure that all staff are trained in care of students with epilepsy and seizure disorders every two years.

title: Voting machines, new-req. to produce voter-verifiable paper record of each vote
summary: Requires new voting machines purchased or leased for use in elections in this State to produce voter-verifiable paper record of each vote cast.

title: School non-instructional staff, retired-permit return, 2 yr, w/out reenrollment
summary: Permits non-instructional staff member retired from PERS to return to employment in school district for up to two years without reenrollment in PERS if employment commences during remainder of 2021-2022 and entirety of 2022-2023 and 2023-2024 school years.*

title: Prosecutor, county-bar from serving in acting capacity for longer than 1 year
summary: Bars individual from serving as county prosecutor in acting capacity for longer than one year, including pending nominee for position.

title: Prosecutor, county-establish residency requirement
summary: Establishes requirement that county prosecutor reside in county of appointment during term of appointment and until qualified successor is appointed.

title: Education relief funds, fed-req 10% to intellectual/developmental disab students
summary: Requires 10 percent of certain federal education relief funds, approximating $26.2 million, be dedicated specifically to students with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

title: Learning loss of developmental disab. students during COVID-19-prepare report
summary: Requires Commissioner of Education to prepare report on learning loss of students with developmental disabilities during COVID-19 public health emergency.

title: Abandoned homes for homeless veterans-authorizes grants to purchase/rehab
summary: Authorizes grants to purchase and rehabilitate abandoned homes for homeless veterans.

title: Tax abatement agreements-municipalities file copies w/in 10 days of execution
summary: Requires municipalities to file copies of tax abatement and exemption agreements with county chief financial officer and county counsel within 10 days of execution.

title: Guardianship of incapacitated persons-req consent to obtain COVID-19 vaccination
summary: Requires each guardian of incapacitated persons to provide consent in order for incapacitated persons to obtain vaccinations and booster vaccinations against COVID-19.

title: Abortion-prohibits use of State funds under certain circumstances
summary: Prohibits use of State funds for termination of pregnancy under certain circumstances.

title: Extraordinary special education State aid-provide 3 yr phase-in of full funding
summary: Provides three-year phase-in of full funding of extraordinary special education State aid.

title: Special education & related svcs.-direct St payment of cost for certain students
summary: Provides for direct State payment of cost of special education and related services for certain students.

title: Board of education meetings-req publicly post full meeting agenda 48 hours prior
summary: Requires board of education to publicly post full meeting agenda 48 hours prior to meeting.

title: Curriculum plans-requires public schools to post online
summary: Requires public schools to post curriculum plans online; requires school districts to make textbooks or other materials used in implementing curriculum plans available for inspection by parents and legal guardians.

title: School pupil transportation pilot program in Ocean County-establishes 3 year
summary: Establishes three-year nonpublic school pupil transportation pilot program in Ocean County to provide funding to county consortium of nonpublic schools that will assume responsibility for mandated nonpublic school busing.

title: St aid reduction-concerns accumulated absences paid to employees upon retirement
summary: Requires State aid reduction to municipalities and school districts by amount of accumulated absences paid to employees upon their retirement.

title: Students, grade 11 & 12-require info on free application for federal student aid
summary: Requires school districts to provide information on free application for federal student aid to students in grades 11 and 12.

title: Pub. sch. teachers w/autistic students-Registered Behavior Technician credential
summary: Requires all public school teachers and paraprofessionals working with autistic students to possess Registered Behavior Technician credential by 2020-2021 school year.

title: Eddie Eagle Gunsafe Program-school districts that incl. grades K through 6 offer
summary: Requires school district that includes grades kindergarten through six to offer Eddie Eagle Gunsafe Program.

title: School board member, participate in volunteer activities-clarifies circumstances
summary: Clarifies circumstances in which school board member may participate in volunteer activities.

title: Safe school resource off/Class Three special law enforcement off-sch dist employ
summary: Permits school districts to employ safe schools resource officers or Class Three special law enforcement officers for security purposes, and requires school districts to have agreement with local law enforcement governing placement of school security personnel.

title: Anti-bullying policy of public schools-extends time for investigation incident
summary: Requires anti-bullying policy of public schools to include provision requiring principal to make preliminary determination and meet with parents of students involved in alleged bullying incident for that determination; extends time for investigating incident.

title: Military member-may qualify for residential tuition rate at county colleges
summary: Allows military member to qualify for resident tuition rate at county college.

title: Anti-bullying policies-mandate additional requirements, expand info DOE guidance
summary: Mandates additional requirements for anti-bullying policies of public schools, expands information provided in DOE guidance document on bullying, and requires parents be provided clear explanation of student rights in school bullying investigations.

title: College Student & Parent Consumer Information Act-applies provisions
summary: Applies provisions of "New Jersey College Student and Parent Consumer Information Act" to proprietary schools and limits eligibility for State student assistance programs to students enrolled in certain proprietary schools.

title: Pension payout options under TPAF, JRS and PERS-requires spousal consent
summary: Requires spousal consent to election of certain pension payout options under TPAF, JRS and PERS.

title: Senior and disabled citizens-increases annual property tax deduction
primary_sponsors: Turner, Shirley K.; Gill, Nia H.
summary: Increases annual property tax deduction for senior citizens and disabled persons from $250 to $500.

title: State Rental Assistance Program;$10.5M
summary: Makes FY 2022 supplemental Grants-in-Aid appropriation of $10.5 million to DCA for State Rental Assistance Program.

title: Business personal property, local exchange telephone companies-local taxation
primary_sponsors: Turner, Shirley K.; Stack, Brian P.
summary: Concerns local taxation of business personal property of local exchange telephone companies.

title: Website, classified-mun and co police departments estab safe areas for purchases
summary: Allows county and municipal police departments to establish designated safe areas for Internet purchase transactions; establishes public awareness campaign.

title: Business prov hiring bonus to targeted employee-allow temp CBT/income tax credit
summary: Allows temporary corporation business tax and gross income tax credits for businesses that provide hiring bonuses to targeted employees.

title: Expungement of criminal records-amends eligibility
summary: Amends eligibility for expungement of criminal records.

title: Extended School Day and School Year Study Commission-establishes
summary: Establishes Extended School Day and School Year Study Commission.

title: Charter school administrators-any superintendent salary caps adopted apply
summary: Provides that any superintendent salary caps adopted by the commissioner will apply to charter school administrators.

title: COVID-19 Vaccine Outreach Commission-establishes;$9M
summary: Establishes the "New Jersey COVID-19 Vaccine Outreach Commission" and appropriates $9 million.

title: Banks and public funds-establishes certain requirements
summary: Establishes certain requirements for banks that serve as depositories of public funds.

title: Fraudulent arbitration agreements, certain-invalid
summary: Provides that certain fraudulent arbitration agreements are invalid.

title: Paperless State government-create task force to study feasibility
summary: Creates task force to study feasibility of paperless State government.

title: Gold Star Family Member-co. clerk or register of deeds/mortgages issue ID card
summary: Permits county clerk or register of deeds and mortgages to issue identification card to Gold Star Family member.

title: Cold War medal-creates
summary: Creates New Jersey Cold War medal.

title: State House grounds-create gallery space for display of certain art works
summary: Creates gallery space on State House grounds for display of certain art works.

title: Gambling, compulsive-makes prevention/ed/treatment prog optional penal, under 21
summary: Makes compulsive gambling prevention, education, and treatment program optional penalty for casino gambling by person under legal age of 21; makes fine optional.*

title: State contracts for medical & dental svcs-permits DOC & DCF to award to vendors
summary: Permits DOC and DCF to award contracts for medical and dental services to vendors.

title: PFRS-allows cert. members to serve until age 70 upon mun governing body approval
summary: Allows certain members of PFRS to serve until age 70 upon approval by municipal governing body.

title: Greenhouse gas emissions-authorizes regulation
summary: Authorizes regulation of greenhouse gas emissions under "Air Pollution Control Act (1954)" and "Global Warming Response Act."

title: Retirement annuities-remove restriction on receipt of by cert members of JRS
summary: Removes restriction on receipt of retirement annuities by certain members of JRS who file for deferred retirement.

title: Emergency responder, volunteer-prov tax credit, service during COVID-19 pandemic
summary: Provides gross income tax credit to volunteer first responders who provided services during COVID-19 pandemic.

title: PERS Prosecutors Part-allow member to estab. purchased PERS credit at addl. cost
summary: Allows members of PERS Prosecutors Part to establish purchased PERS credit in the Part at additional cost.

title: Unemployment benefits-concerns eligibility
summary: Concerns eligibility for unemployment benefits.

title: Diapers-requires insurance coverage of diapers when medically necessary
summary: Requires insurance coverage of diapers when medically necessary.

title: Lead Education Accountability & Disclosure Act-pub. water systems prov cert info
summary: Requires public water systems to provide customers with certain information regarding lead in drinking water.

title: Treatment of substance abuse disorders, cert.-req. health insurers prov coverage
summary: Requires certain health insurers to provide coverage for counseling and behavioral therapies for any person receiving medication-assisted treatment.

title: Unemployment compensation overpayments-concerns repayment
summary: Concerns repayment of unemployment compensation overpayments.

title: Equine therapy or rehabilitation-health insurers provide coverage
summary: Requires health insurers to provide coverage for equine therapy or rehabilitation under certain circumstances.

title: Water testing and filters-water supplier reimburse residential customers
summary: Requires water suppliers to reimburse residential customers for drinking water testing under certain circumstances.

title: Water Data Center-establishes at public institution of higher education;$1M
summary: Establishes NJ Water Data Center at public institution of higher education; appropriates $1 million.

title: Epinephrine auto-injector device & asthma inhaler-req health insur prov coverage
primary_sponsors: Vitale, Joseph F.; Pou, Nellie; Singleton, Troy
summary: Requires health insurance carriers to provide coverage for epinephrine auto-injector devices and asthma inhalers; limits cost sharing for health insurance coverage of insulin.

title: Prescr drug supply chain-estab data reporting req; report on trends in pricing
primary_sponsors: Singleton, Troy; Vitale, Joseph F.; Pou, Nellie
summary: Establishes certain data reporting requirements for prescription drug supply chain; establishes Drug Affordability Council; appropriates $1,500,000.*

title: Pharmacy benefits manager business practices-estab new transparency standards
primary_sponsors: Vitale, Joseph F.; Singleton, Troy; Pou, Nellie
summary: Establishes new transparency standards for pharmacy benefits manager business practices.

title: Ultrasounds on pregnant women-performed by licensed health care professional
summary: Requires certain ultrasounds on pregnant women to be performed by licensed health care professionals.

title: One-time payment program-establish for aid for certain eligible taxpayers;$35M
summary: Establishes one-time payment program in Department of Treasury for aid for certain eligible taxpayers; appropriates $35 million.

title: Fruit and vegetable donations-provides CBT and income tax credits for value
primary_sponsors: Ruiz, M. Teresa; Oroho, Steven V.
summary: Provides corporation business tax and gross income tax credits for value of certain fruit and vegetable donations made by commercial farm operators.

title: Medical Reserve Corp volunteers-provides income tax deduction, serve 150 hours
summary: Provides gross income tax deduction for volunteers of New Jersey Medical Reserve Corps that serve at least 150 hours during public health emergency.

title: State agency websites-make accessible to people w/disabilities on mobile devices
summary: Requires State agencies to make their websites accessible to people with disabilities and on mobile devices.

title: Bias intimidation law-makes various changes
summary: Makes various changes to bias intimidation law.

title: Mental illness/substance use disorder-clarify resid w/special needs, add to list
summary: Clarifies that residents with mental illness or substance use disorders may be included on public safety special assistance municipal lists.

title: Victim of sexual assault-establish right to notify developments in criminal case
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; O'Scanlon, Declan J.
summary: Establishes right of sexual assault victim to notification of certain developments in criminal case.

title: Identity theft victims-petition for judicial determination of factual innocence
summary: Allows identity theft victims to petition for judicial determination of factual innocence.

title: Law enforce.-clarify off. appointed by ed. instit & NJT, entitled to cert rights
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Madden, Fred H.
summary: Clarifies that law enforcement officers appointed by educational institutions and NJT are entitled to certain statutory rights and privileges.

title: Filing false report against law enforcement officers-establish crime
summary: Establishes crime of knowingly filing false report against law enforcement officers.

title: Ancient Order of Hibernians-authorizes special license plate
summary: Authorizes special Ancient Order of Hibernians license plate.

title: Child sexual abuse-establishes rebuttable presumption of pretrial detention
summary: Establishes rebuttable presumption of pretrial detention for child sexual abuse.

title: Halfway houses-req DOC report cert info, concerning resid community release prog
summary: Requires DOC to report certain information concerning halfway houses.

title: Disab. persons-establish program to connect with job training & employment
summary: Establishes program in DOLWD to connect persons with disabilities with job training and employment.

title: Pharmacy benefits manager-prohibit requiring covered persons to use mail service
summary: Prohibits pharmacy benefits managers from requiring covered persons to use mail service pharmacies.

title: MVC online transactions-require to operate hotline to assist with
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Bucco, Anthony M.
summary: Requires MVC to operate call center to assist with online transactions.

title: Transportation network companies-share info., sexual misconduct investigation
primary_sponsors: Greenstein, Linda R.; Corrado, Kristin M.
summary: Requires transportation network companies to share information concerning sexual misconduct investigation of driver; authorizes transportation network company to ban drivers from accessing digital network during and following investigation.

title: Access Link service-NJT establish program to provide free rides
summary: Requires NJT to establish pilot program to provide free rides on rail, light rail, and bus service to individuals eligible for Access Link service.

title: State school aid-provides additional districts experiencing enrollment increases
summary: Provides additional State school aid to school districts experiencing enrollment increases due to conversion of age-restricted housing developments to non-restricted developments.

title: Autism Education Council-establishes
summary: Establishes Autism Education Council.

title: Tuition & fee incr, higher ed instit-submit to Secretary of Higher Ed for review
summary: Requires public institutions of higher education to submit proposed tuition and fees for upcoming academic year to Secretary of Higher Education for review.

title: Manufacturing sector-design a manuf. career pathway to provide students w/skills
summary: Requires Secretary of Higher Education, in consultation with various entities, to design  manufacturing career pathway to provide students with skills necessary to gain employment in manufacturing sector.

title: Military Dependents Scholarships-extends elig, approved career/technical ed prog
summary: Extends eligibility for Military Dependents Scholarships to students enrolled in approved career and technical education programs and to dependents of certain military members who served in Operation Freedom's Sentinel and Operation Inherent Resolve.

title: Innovate NJ Act-establishes tax-free business incubators at instit. of higher ed
summary: "Innovate New Jersey Act"; establishes tax-free business incubators at institutions of higher education.

title: Cash assistance benefits under Work First program-revises requirements
summary: Revises requirements for cash assistance benefits under Work First New Jersey program.

title: Higher Education Student Advisory Commission-estab. to advise on student issues
summary: Establishes commission to advise Secretary of Higher Education on students' issues of concern.

title: Financial aid, federal-estab 3-yr pilot prog, provide students info & assistance
summary: Establishes three-year pilot program in DOE to provide certain students with information and assistance regarding federal financial aid application.

title: Outmigration of high school graduates to out-of-State institutions-conduct study
summary: Requires Secretary of Higher Education to conduct study to determine causes of outmigration of high school graduates to out-of-State institutions of higher education.

title: Easy Enrollment Health Insurance Program-establishes
summary: Establishes New Jersey Easy Enrollment Health Insurance Program.

title: Alcohol manuf licenses-permit holders to offer retail memberships to customers
summary: Permits holders of certain alcohol manufacturing licenses to offer retail memberships to customers.

title: School drinking water-test and remediate lead and disclose results
summary: Requires public and nonpublic schools to test for and remediate lead in drinking water, and disclose test results.

title: Investment Council, State-concerns certain reports; report cert. info. quarterly
summary: Requires State Investment Council report certain information quarterly; requires council's regulations addressing political contributions apply to federal or national committees and non-State political committees.

title: Professional service contract-award to bi-state authorities, competitive process
summary: Requires contracts for professional services be awarded by certain bi-state authorities pursuant to competitive contracting process.

title: Homeowners History Property Reinvestment Act-claim tax credit for rehabilitating
primary_sponsors: Turner, Shirley K.; Ruiz, M. Teresa
summary: "Homeowners' Historic Property Reinvestment Act"; allows homeowners to claim credit against gross income tax for certain costs of rehabilitating historic properties.

title: Child support-estab greater court discretion, license suspension & credit report
summary: Establishes greater discretion in court concerning child support related license suspension and credit reporting.

title: Auto. insur territorial rating plan, priv passenger-not place exposure, ZIP code
summary: Provides that private passenger automobile insurance rating territories shall not place exposure in territory based on ZIP code under certain circumstances.

title: Military personnel-extend drivers lic, ID card & motor veh regis expiration date
summary: Clarifies extension of driver's license, identification card, and motor vehicle registration expiration dates for certain military personnel.

title: Students residing in tax-exempt housing-concerns educational costs
summary: Requires nonpublic secondary schools and independent institutions of higher education to make payment to school districts for educational costs of students residing in tax-exempt housing upon adoption of resolution by municipal governing body.

title: Death certificate-St registrar permit county surrogate to provide certified copy
summary: Requires State registrar to permit county surrogate to provide certified copy of death certificate to authorized person under certain circumstances.

title: Sch discipline practice incl racial disparities/effectiveness task force-examine
summary: Establishes task force to examine school discipline practices including racial disparities and effectiveness.

title: Schools, private postsecondary-establishes tuition reimbursement program
summary: Establishes tuition reimbursement program for students enrolled in certain private postsecondary educational schools.

title: Teachers, public-biennially complete 2 hrs., cultural competence prof. devel.
summary: Requires teachers to biennially complete two hours of professional development related to cultural competence.

title: Male Teachers of Color Mentorship Pilot Program-establishes;$50K
summary: Establishes "Male Teachers of Color Mentorship Pilot Program"; appropriates $95,000.*

title: School Meal Fund-sch district establish to assist student w/meal bill in arrears
summary: Requires school district to establish "School Meal Fund" to assist students with school meal bill in arrears.

title: Youth Suicide Prevention Advisory Council-prepare prevention instruction report
primary_sponsors: Ruiz, M. Teresa; Codey, Richard J.; Lampitt, Pamela R.
summary: Requires NJ Youth Suicide Prevention Advisory Council to prepare report regarding suicide prevention instruction in public schools.

title: Summer Earn & Learn Pilot Program-estab.; concerns access to suppl learning asst
summary: Establishes "Summer Earn and Learn Pilot Program."

title: Educational Opportunity Fund-Higher Education Secretary create/maintain database
primary_sponsors: Ruiz, M. Teresa; Cunningham, Sandra B.
summary: Requires Secretary of Higher Education to create and maintain database of Educational Opportunity Fund student admissions.

title: Landlord-tenant court records-establishes confidentiality
primary_sponsors: Codey, Richard J.; Stack, Brian P.
summary: Establishes confidentiality of landlord-tenant court records; addresses adverse actions on rental applications.

title: Evictions-enhances use of breach of implied warranty of habitability defense
primary_sponsors: Codey, Richard J.; Stack, Brian P.
summary: Codifies and enhances use of breach of implied warranty of habitability as defense to certain eviction actions.

title: Generic prescription drugs-prohibits excessive increases in prices charged
summary: Prohibits excessive increases in prices charged for essential off-patent and generic prescription drugs and biological products.

title: Cemetery companies-estab minimum dollar amount required in Maintenance/Preserv
summary: Establishes minimum dollar amount required in Maintenance and Preservation Funds of cemetery companies established before December 1, 1971.

title: Zero-Based Budget Act-State Treasurer develop/integrate cert practices/procedure
summary: "Zero-Based Budget Act;" requires State Treasurer to develop and integrate certain zero-based budgeting practices and procedures in preparation and submission of Governor's annual budget message.

title: School Safety & Security Act-establishes penalties for criminal acts on sch prop
primary_sponsors: Oroho, Steven V.; O'Scanlon, Declan J.
summary: The "School Safety and Security Act"; establishes penalties for certain criminal acts committed on school property.

title: School districts, Highlands Region-revises State school aid reductions schedule
summary: Revises schedule of State school aid reduction for school districts in Highlands Region.

title: Death penalty for certain murders-reinstates
summary: Reinstates the death penalty for certain murders.

title: Sch dist, Type II-hold Nov sch election to receive voter approval of base budget
summary: Requires Type II school district without board of school estimate holding its school election in November to receive voter approval of base budget.

title: Sex offender-require local government registration, prohibit living near schools
summary: Requires local government notification of sex offender registration and prohibits sex offenders from living near schools, child care centers or playgrounds.

title: Remuneration paid to employee-require public employers provide annual statement
summary: Requires public employers to provide each employee with annual written statement of all remuneration paid to, or on behalf of, that employee by that public employer.

title: Correctional police officers & body worn cameras-require to wear
primary_sponsors: Ruiz, M. Teresa; Greenstein, Linda R.
summary: Requires correctional police officers to wear body worn cameras and security camera systems to be installed in State correctional facilities.

title: Working Class Families Anti-Hunger Act-req school prov free breakfasts & lunches
primary_sponsors: Ruiz, M. Teresa; Vitale, Joseph F.
summary: Requires schools to provide free school breakfasts and lunches to students from working class, middle-income families; designated as "Working Class Families' Anti-Hunger Act."

title: Base realignment and closure-requires annual General Fund appropriation
summary: Requires annual General Fund appropriation to DMVA for purposes of base realignment and closure.

title: Environmental infra. proj-req Infra Bank prov incentives, employ local residents
summary: Requires DEP to prioritize funding for environmental infrastructure projects for applicants with established program to employ, at project or related facilities, local residents or residents of nearby urban aid qualifying municipalities.

title: SNAP Emp & Training Prog-designate community college provider of allowable svc
primary_sponsors: Pou, Nellie; Ruiz, M. Teresa; Murphy, Carol A.
summary: Designates each community college in State as provider of allowable services under SNAP employment and training program.

title: Diversity in membership-req to be considered for appointment to cert boards/comm
summary: Requires racial and gender diversity in membership to be considered for appointments to certain boards and commissions established by statute.

title: School buses, Type S-permits certain persons to drive
primary_sponsors: Beach, James; Singer, Robert W.; Thompson, Samuel D.
summary: Permits certain persons to drive Type S school buses.

title: Highland Water Protection & Planning Act-suspends until cert. conditions are met
summary: Suspends "Highlands Water Protection and Planning Act" until certain conditions are met.

title: Commercial corridors, Highlands Region-prov. municipal planning auth to develop
summary: Provides "Highlands Water Protection and Planning Act" exemption, and clarifies municipal planning or zoning authority, for certain development along commercial corridors in Highlands Region.

title: Freshwater fishing/hunting/trapping-Fish/Game Council sole authority to regulate
summary: Clarifies that Fish and Game Council has sole authority to regulate freshwater fishing, hunting, and trapping.

title: DEP lands for fishing, hunting and trapping purposes-provide for no net loss
summary: Provides for no net loss of DEP lands for fishing, hunting, and trapping purposes.

title: Voter identity proof-certain documentation required
summary: Requires certain documentation as proof of voter identity to vote; updates procedures for challenging voters regarding proof of identity.

title: Law enforcement officer retired due to disability-tuition assistance eligible
primary_sponsors: Connors, Christopher J.; Corrado, Kristin M.
summary: Provides that former law enforcement officers who retired due to disability are eligible to receive tuition assistance to attend public institution of higher education or proprietary institution licensed to confer academic degrees.

summary: Reestablishes senior citizen supplemental stabilization aid.

title: Breann's Law-children w/catastrophic illnesses provide out of network coverage
summary: "Breann's Law" requires health insurers, SHBP and NJ FamilyCare to provide "out of network" coverage for children with catastrophic illnesses.

title: Intoxicated employees-prevented from receiving worker's compensation
summary: Prevents intoxicated employees from receiving workers' compensation.

title: Sales receipts-requires account number to be truncated
summary: Requires account numbers to be truncated on certain sales receipts.

title: Motor vehicle manufacturer rebates-excl. value from sales price under sales tax
summary: Excludes value of certain manufacturer rebates from sales price of motor vehicles taxable under sales and use tax.

title: Energy tax revenue-caps St use portion, ensures balance paid annually as mun aid
summary: Caps State use portion of energy tax revenues and ensures balance of such revenues are paid annually as municipal aid.

title: Vol firefighters, rescue & first aid squad memb-allows $5K income tax deduction
primary_sponsors: Gopal, Vin; Connors, Christopher J.; Corrado, Kristin M.
summary: Allows certain volunteer firefighters, rescue and first aid squad members to claim $2,000 gross income tax exemption.*

title: Veteran's gross income tax exemption-extends to deceased veteran spouses
primary_sponsors: Connors, Christopher J.; Gill, Nia H.
summary: Extends veteran's gross income tax exemption to spouses of deceased veterans.

title: Fuel taxes, paid on motor fuel for motor vehicles personal use-tax deduction
summary: Provides gross income tax deduction for New Jersey fuel taxes paid through purchases of motor fuel for personal use of motor vehicles.

title: Superstorm Sandy aid overpayments-establish process for recovery
summary: Establishes process for recovery of Superstorm Sandy aid overpayments and makes General Fund appropriation.

title: Spent nuclear fuel from decommissioned nuclear power plant-deems real property
summary: Deems spent nuclear fuel from decommissioned nuclear power plant to be real property.

title: Nuclear power fac, decommissioned-req community svc pymt in lieu of prop tax
summary: Requires decommissioned nuclear power facility to pay annual community service payment in lieu of property taxes.

title: Nuclear power plant, decomm-subject spent nuclear fuel to tax, bus personal prop
summary: Subjects spent nuclear fuel located in a decommissioned nuclear power plant to taxation as business personal property.

title: Deferred compensation-exclude public school/federal tax-exempt org. employee
summary: Excludes deferred compensation of certain public school and federal tax-exempt organization employees from current taxation under the gross income tax.

title: Farm to Public Institution of Higher Education Program-establish
summary: Establishes "New Jersey Farm to Public Institution of Higher Education Program."

title: Mil personnel killed in act of duty-prov free tuition, surviving spouse/children
primary_sponsors: Oroho, Steven V.; Corrado, Kristin M.
summary: Provides free tuition at public institutions of higher education for surviving spouses and dependent children of military personnel killed in performance of duties.

title: Internet gaming, inactive accounts-abandoned unclaimed property after 3 years
summary: Provides amounts remaining in inactive Internet gaming accounts will be abandoned property after three years under unclaimed property law.

title: Animal control funds-concerns distribution and use of unexpended
summary: Requires transfer of certain excess animal control funds to prosecutors, municipalities, police departments, and Police Training Commission for enforcement of animal cruelty laws and training humane law enforcement officers.

title: Domestic violence-related assault-upgades under certain circumstances
summary: Upgrades domestic violence-related assault under certain circumstances.

title: Victim Info. & Notification Everyday System-notify domestic viol victims through
summary: Requires notification of certain domestic violence victims through the Victim Information and Notification Everyday System (VINE).

title: Flexible spending account-req same limitation prov by law adjusted for inflation
summary: Requires same limitation on public employee contributions to flexible spending accounts as provided by federal law adjusted for inflation.

title: Governor, State Legislature-estab. ranked-choice voting procedure for elections
summary: Establishes ranked-choice voting procedure for elections for Governor, State Senate, State General Assembly, United States Senate and House of Representatives, and presidential primaries and general elections for electors for United States President and Vice-President.

title: SNAP-requires standard certification period of 12 and 24 months
summary: Requires standard certification period of 12 and 24 months, under certain circumstances, within SNAP.

title: County improvement authority contracting powers-concerns
summary: Concerns county improvement authority contracting powers.

title: Facial recognition technology-restricts use by governmental entities
summary: Restricts use of facial recognition technology and other biometric recognition by governmental entities.

title: Teachers loan redemption program-estab to Assist State Students Loan Program
summary: Establishes loan redemption program for teachers to redeem loan amounts received under the New Jersey College Loans to Assist State Students Loan Program through employment in public or nonpublic schools in New Jersey.

title: State assessment-allow parent/guardian exclude student from administration of
summary: Allows parent or guardian to exclude student from administration of State assessment.

title: School bus contractor-prov info, result of criminal background check on bus driv
summary: Requires school bus contractor to provide information to board of education on results of criminal background checks of all school bus drivers assigned routes in district pursuant to contract.

title: Asthma-friendly schools-boards of education required to adopt policies
summary: Requires boards of education to adopt policies to make schools asthma-friendly.

title: Student participation, certain assessments-post on their websites
summary: Requires school districts and DOE to post on their websites information regarding student participation in certain assessments.

title: Charter school application-provide local board of education w/approval authority
summary: Provides local board of education with approval authority of charter school application.

title: School district tax levy cap-prov adjustment, incr in special ed per pupil costs
summary: Provides an adjustment to school district tax levy cap for increases in special education costs per pupil in excess of $40,000 that exceed such prior year costs by more than 2%.

title: High sch graduate-conduct study, extent/cause of outmigration to out-of-St coll
summary: Directs Secretary of Higher Education to conduct study to determine extent and cause of outmigration of high school graduates to out-of-State colleges.

title: Election workers-permits members of National Guard to be, certain circumstances
summary: Permits members of NJ National Guard to be election workers under certain circumstances.

title: Medical alert devices and services-exempts from sales and use tax
summary: Exempts medical alert devices and services from sales and use tax.

title: Children, Youth, & Families Bill of Rights-recognize & protect certain rights
summary: "Establishes Children, Youth, and Families Bill of Rights" to recognize and protect certain rights for children, youth, families.

title: Franklinite-designates as State official mineral
primary_sponsors: Oroho, Steven V.; Durr, Edward; Space, Parker
summary: Designates franklinite as official mineral of State of NJ.

title: Anti-Semitism Task Force-creates
primary_sponsors: Testa, Michael L.; Singer, Robert W.

title: Farmers, beginning-CBT credit to person leasing agriculture lands
primary_sponsors: Oroho, Steven V.; Cruz-Perez, Nilsa I.
summary: Provides corporation business tax and gross income tax credits to persons leasing agricultural land to beginning farmers.

title: Professional license-issue for certain individuals w/good standing licenses
summary: Requires professional licensing boards to issue licenses for certain individuals with good standing licenses or certification in other jurisdictions under certain circumstances.

title: Higher Education Student Advisory Commission-establish
primary_sponsors: Polistina, Vincent J.; Cunningham, Sandra B.
summary: Establishes New Jersey Higher Education Student Advisory Commission.

title: Supporting Educational & Entrepreneurial Development Program-establish
primary_sponsors: Polistina, Vincent J.; Cunningham, Sandra B.
summary: Establishes "Supporting Educational and Entrepreneurial Development (S.E.E.D.) Program" in Office of Secretary of Higher Education.

title: Federal financial aid guidance-establish pilot program
primary_sponsors: Polistina, Vincent J.; Cunningham, Sandra B.
summary: Establishes pilot program to provide guidance to students applying for federal financial aid.

title: Public Svc Loan Forgiveness prog-concerning the provision of information about

summary: Requires institutions of higher education and government employers to provide certain students and employees with information about Public Service Loan Forgiveness program.

title: Better Education Savings Trust prog-improves management & administration
primary_sponsors: Oroho, Steven V.; Cunningham, Sandra B.

title: Chiara's Law-proh euthanizing animals in shelter except for health/safety reason
summary: "Chiara's Law"; prohibits euthanizing animals in shelters or pounds except for health or safety reasons.

title: Higher Education Funding Formula Commission-establishes
primary_sponsors: Polistina, Vincent J.; Cunningham, Sandra B.
summary: Establishes "New Jersey Higher Education Funding Formula Commission."

title: COVID-19 PTSD programs & services-estab pub awareness camp for frontline workers
summary: Establishes public awareness campaign concerning programs and services for first responders, healthcare workers, other frontline workers, and their families experiencing mental health issues related to COVID-19 pandemic.

summary: Requires DOH to provide information regarding spina bifida to certain parents and families of newborns; "Levi's Law."

summary: Requires DHS to provide psychiatric service dogs to first responders, health care workers, and other frontline workers suffering from post-traumatic stress disorders due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

summary: Increases amount of cigarette and other tobacco product tax revenues provided to New Jersey Commission on Cancer Research from $1 million to $4 million; establishes dedicated, non-lapsing Cancer Research Fund.

title: Emergency medical services certification-requires temporary issuance
summary: Requires issuance of temporary emergency medical services certifications.

title: CDS misuse-create a registry of individuals who req medical assistance as result
summary: Creates registry of individuals who required medical assistance as result of misusing controlled dangerous substance.

title: COVID-19 Frontline Mental Health Claims Program-establishes; makes approp.

title: Volunteer Medical Professional Health Care Act-concerns immunity for civil liab.

title: Pharmacists-dispense certain drugs in emergency without prescription
summary: Permits pharmacists to dispense certain drugs in emergency without prescription.

title: Cancer Research from Workforce Development Partnership Fd-prov dedicated funding
summary: Provides dedicated funding for New Jersey Commission on Cancer Research from Workforce Development Partnership Fund.

title: SNAP participation-certain students exempt from 20 hour weekly work requirement
summary: Provides for student recipients of educational opportunity funds to be exempt from 20 hour weekly work requirement ordinarily required for student SNAP participation.

title: Home repair and functional improvements-allows income tax deduction

title: COVID-19 spread at swimming pools, planned real estate devel.-establish immunity

summary: Establishes immunity relating to COVID-19 spread at swimming pools in planned real estate developments.

title: Election ballots-clarify Spanish/English accessibility req applicable printing
summary: Clarifies bilingual Spanish and English language accessibility requirements applicable to printing primary and general election ballots in certain districts.

title: State innovation economy-Secretary of State develop national marketing campaign
summary: Directs Secretary of State to contract with qualified vendor to develop national marketing campaign promoting State's innovation economy; appropriates $1,000,000. *

title: Libraries-allows disposal of certain materials without advertising for bids
summary: Allows public libraries, boards of education, and local governments to dispose of certain personal property without advertising for bids.

title: Digital Asset and Blockchain Technology Act
summary: "Digital Asset and Blockchain Technology Act."

title: Consumer reports-req. reporting agencies report number of free reports annually
summary: Requires reporting agencies to report to Division of Consumer Affairs number of free consumer reports furnished to New Jersey resident consumers annually.

summary: Prohibits consumer reporting agencies from making reports containing adverse information related to employees affected by federal government shutdown.

title: Energy usage data, customer-estab standards concerning electric pub util use of
summary: Directs BPU to establish certain standards concerning electric public utility use of customer energy usage data.

title: Veteran-DMVA notify certain organizations, name of deceased and killed in action
summary: Requires DMVA notify certain veteran organizations and county officers of names of persons buried or cremated in State veteran cemeteries.

summary: Requires health care facilities to screen health care workers and first responders for symptoms of post-traumatic stress disorder related to COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Mandated Health Benefits Advisory Commission-study finan impact of mandated benf

summary: Requires Mandated Health Benefits Advisory Commission to study financial impact of all enacted mandated health benefits.

title: Petitions of nomination of candidates for committees-req functioning e-mail
summary: Requires petitions of nomination of candidates for State and county committees of political parties to include functioning e-mail addresses.

title: Accessibility signage-revises requirement, indicate for people with disabilities
summary: Revises requirements for signage indicating accessible facilities for people with disabilities.

title: Pandemic PTSD Task Force, temporary-establishes
summary: Establishes temporary Pandemic PTSD Task Force.

title: PTSD & coronavirus-establish toll free helpline for frontline workers
summary: Establishes toll free helpline for first responders, health care workers, and other frontline workers experiencing post-traumatic stress disorder.

title: Urgent care & retail health clinics-defibrillator on site & emp. trained to use
summary: Requires urgent care and retail health clinics to have defibrillator on site and employees trained to use defibrillator.

title: Bear spray-allows possession under certain conditions
summary: Allows possession of bear spray under certain conditions.

title: ID card fees-exempt persons w/disabilities & homeless persons from payment
summary: Exempts persons with disabilities and persons who are homeless from payment of identification card fees.

title: Drivers, licensing-permits certain drivers' schools to conduct road tests
summary: Permits certain drivers' schools to conduct road tests.

summary: Exempts certain non-profit entities from requirement that certain employers offer employees certain pre-tax transportation fringe benefits.

summary: Allows gross income tax refunds to be credited against taxpayer's delinquent local property taxes.

summary: Removes stump grinding as service requiring licensure by New Jersey Board of Tree Experts.

summary: Appropriates $30 million to EDA from federal "Coronavirus Relief Fund" to assist certain live entertainment venues.

summary: Upgrades burglary of a residence as a crime of the second degree; upgrades it to a crime of the first degree if committed while armed.

summary: Broadens riot and disorderly conduct; enhances penalties for public monument destruction; addresses riot victim assaults; creates crime of promotion of violent, disorderly assembly.

summary: Excludes discounts for services rendered from gross income.

title: Capital Improvement Assistance Program-supplemental appropriation to HMFA;$25M
primary_sponsors: Cryan, Joseph P.; Scutari, Nicholas P.; Benson, Daniel R.

title: Cats and other animals-prohibits surgical declawing
summary: Prohibits surgical declawing of cats and other animals.

title: Foreclosure action-clarify exceptions, req. for person seeking to be made party
summary: Clarifies exceptions regarding requirement for person seeking to be made party in certain foreclosure actions.

summary: Requires New Jersey Historical Commission to establish Black Heritage Trail; permits New Jersey Black Cultural and Heritage Initiative Foundation to use funds for markers on certain trail sites; appropriates $1 million.*

title: Federal jobs and investment in the State-creates office to encourage creation
summary: Creates office to encourage creation of federal jobs and federal infrastructure investment in the State.

title: Veteran owned businesses-waives business formation and registration fees

title: Domestic violence-adds coercive control to definition

summary: Adds coercive control to definition of domestic violence.

title: Pretrial release violations-clarify penalties; provide written notice to victim
summary: Clarifies penalties for certain violations of pretrial release; directs prosecutor to provide written notice of release to victim.

title: Street paving costs, qualified private communities-mun. required to reimburse
summary: Requires municipalities to reimburse qualified private communities for street paving costs.

title: Tax board, county-permits board of chosen freeholders appoint interim member
summary: Permits county board of chosen freeholders to appoint interim member of county tax board under certain circumstances.

title: Real estate devel., new-requires installation of emergency power to common areas
summary: Requires installation of emergency power supply systems to certain common areas of new planned real estate developments; provides related tax incentives.

title: Just compensation-authorizes payment to bus., losses due to pub. health emerg.
summary: Authorizes payment of just compensation to businesses for losses due to public health emergency closures.

title: Menstrual hygiene products-estab pilot prog covering under Medicaid & FamilyCare

summary: Requires DHS to establish a pilot program covering menstrual hygiene products under Medicaid and NJ FamilyCare.

summary: Permits certain businesses to complete business registration certificate and annual report together.

title: Inmates, county-req subsistence vouchers be accepted in every county
summary: Requires subsistence vouchers issued to county inmates upon release be accepted in every county.

title: Crime witness, cert-auth funding to protect; expand Crime Victim's Bill of Right
summary: Authorizes funding to protect safety of cooperating witnesses; expands Crime Victim's Bill of Rights to provide these witnesses with certain guarantees.

title: Domestic violence restraining orders-seizure of ammunition/firearm components
summary: Requires seizure of ammunition and certain firearm components in response to domestic violence restraining order or conviction.

title: Silver Alert Sys, missing person w/dementia-same broadcast alerts as Amber Alert
summary: Requires Silver Alert System receive same broadcast alerts as Amber Alert System.

title: Substance abuse disorder-incl. in list that qualify student for State special ed
summary: Includes substance abuse disorder in list of disabilities that qualify student for State special education and related services.

title: Investment metal bullion & cert investment coins-exempts from sales and use tax

summary: Exempts sales of investment metal bullion and certain investment coins from sales and use tax.

title: Petroleum products, certain-decreases gross receipts tax rate
summary: Decreases petroleum products gross receipts tax rate on certain petroleum products.

title: Domestic viol screening-obstetrical provider perform; prevent certain disclosure
summary: Requires certain providers to perform intimate partner violence screenings and all health care professionals to take certain actions to prevent perpetrator of intimate partner violence from obtaining copies of victim's medical record.*

title: Retirement income exclusion-modify eligibility requirements, other income source

title: Paraffin-excl use in manuf of candles from petroleum products gross receipts tax
summary: Excludes paraffin used in manufacture of candles from petroleum products gross receipts tax.

title: Health savings accounts, individual-extends federal income tax advantages

title: Voter, ineligible non-citizen voter registrants-remove from system
summary: Provides for agreement between Secretary of State and AOC to match information in Statewide voter registration system with certain jury records in order to identify and remove ineligible non-citizen voter registrants from system.

title: Mercury free-new flooring for schools and child care centers be certified
summary: Requires new flooring for schools and child care centers to be certified mercury free.

title: Motor vehicles-establishes electronic lien and titling system

title: Liquor license, inactive-provide for transfer and sale, qualify smart growth mun

title: SNAP eligibility req.-revises for certain students in higher education instit.

title: NJ FamilyCare & individual health coverage/small emp health benf prog-correction

title: Alco beverage sale-allow co/mun enter revenue sharing agreement by permit holder

title: Disabled person's residence-mun establish reserved parking zone on adjacent prop
